elements.content:
Request Starts Here

Every action begins on the phone and enters through one front door: the gateway.

- Mobile app sends HTTPS request
- Gateway is the public entry point
- Routes requests to internal services
Request Starts Here

The Gateway Guards the Door

Before routing deeper, the gateway checks whether the request is allowed to continue.

- Rate limit protects capacity
- Auth checks the token
- Bad traffic stops early
The Gateway Guards the Door

Auth Turns Tokens Into Identity

A valid token becomes user identity. An invalid token gets rejected before touching product data.

- Token is verified
- User ID is attached
- Invalid requests are denied
Auth Turns Tokens Into Identity

Creating a Post

The post service writes durable data, stores media separately, then announces that something changed.

- Text goes to the database
- Images/videos go to object storage
- An event updates downstream systems
Creating a Post

graph LR
  Client["Mobile app"] --> Gateway["API gateway"]
  Gateway --> FeedService["Feed service"]
  FeedService --> Cache["Feed cache"]
  FeedService --> PostDB["Post database"]
  Cache --> FeedService
  PostDB --> FeedService
  FeedService --> Response["Feed response"]
  Response --> Client

  classDef base fill:#111827,stroke:#334155,color:#e5e7eb,stroke-width:1px;
  classDef cache fill:#083344,stroke:#22d3ee,color:#ffffff,stroke-width:3px;
  class Client,Gateway,FeedService,Cache,PostDB,Response base;

Reading the Feed

Feeds need to feel instant, so the service checks fast storage before doing heavier database work.

- Cache handles hot feeds
- Database is the source of truth
- Service assembles the response
Reading the Feed

Data Shapes the Product

Users, posts, comments, and follows form the core graph behind the feed.

- Indexes speed common lookups
- Follows decide relevance
- Comments attach to posts

graph TD
  UserTable["Users"] -->|"writes"| PostTable["Posts"]
  UserTable -->|"writes"| CommentTable["Comments"]
  CommentTable -->|"belongs to"| PostTable
  UserTable -->|"follows"| FollowTable["Follows"]
  FollowTable --> FeedQuery["Feed query"]
  PostTable --> FeedQuery
  Indexes["Indexes"] --> FeedQuery

  classDef base fill:#111827,stroke:#334155,color:#e5e7eb,stroke-width:1px;
  classDef data fill:#172554,stroke:#60a5fa,color:#ffffff,stroke-width:3px;
  class UserTable,PostTable,CommentTable,FollowTable,FeedQuery,Indexes base;
Data Shapes the Product

graph LR
  MediaStorage["Object storage"] --> CDN["Content delivery network"]
  CDN --> EdgeUS["Edge server"]
  CDN --> EdgeEU["Edge server"]
  EdgeUS --> Client["Mobile app"]
  EdgeEU --> Client
  Realtime["Real-time updates"] --> Client
  PostService["Post service"] --> MediaStorage
  PostService --> Realtime

  classDef base fill:#111827,stroke:#334155,color:#e5e7eb,stroke-width:1px;
  classDef media fill:#422006,stroke:#facc15,color:#ffffff,stroke-width:3px;
  class MediaStorage,CDN,EdgeUS,EdgeEU,Client,Realtime,PostService base;

Media Takes the Fast Lane

Images and videos avoid the app servers when possible and ship from edge locations close to the user.

- Object storage holds originals
- CDN edge serves copies
- Live updates notify the client
Media Takes the Fast Lane

Scale Without Melting

Stateless services can be duplicated, while queues absorb spikes and retries avoid lost work.

- Multiple identical instances
- Queue smooths traffic bursts
- Idempotency prevents duplicates

graph LR
  Gateway["API gateway"] --> LoadBalancer["Load balancer"]
  LoadBalancer --> ServiceA["Service instance"]
  LoadBalancer --> ServiceB["Service instance"]
  LoadBalancer --> ServiceC["Service instance"]
  ServiceA --> Queue["Work queue"]
  ServiceB --> Queue
  ServiceC --> Queue
  Queue --> Worker["Background worker"]
  Worker --> Store["Database"]
  Worker -."retry safely".-> Queue

  classDef base fill:#111827,stroke:#334155,color:#e5e7eb,stroke-width:1px;
  classDef scale fill:#064e3b,stroke:#34d399,color:#ffffff,stroke-width:3px;
  class Gateway,LoadBalancer,ServiceA,ServiceB,ServiceC,Queue,Worker,Store base;
Scale Without Melting

Production Checklist

A social app is fast when the architecture is boring in the right places.

- Deploy across regions for lower latency
- Monitor errors, latency, and traffic spikes
- Use canary rollouts before full releases
- Keep auth and rate limiting strict
Production Checklist
